text_original: For regulating the human (in our constitution) and rendering the (proper) service to the heavenly, there is nothing like moderation.
text_normalized: For regulating the human (in our constitution) and rendering the (proper) service to the heavenly, there is nothing like moderation.

text_original: It is only by this moderation that there is effected an early return (to man's normal state).
text_normalized: It is only by this moderation that there is effected an early return (to man's normal state).

text_original: That early return is what I call the repeated accumulation of the attributes (of the Dao).
text_normalized: That early return is what I call the repeated accumulation of the attributes (of the Dao).

text_original: With that repeated accumulation of those attributes, there comes the subjugation (of every obstacle to such return).
text_normalized: With that repeated accumulation of those attributes, there comes the subjugation (of every obstacle to such return).

text_original: Of this subjugation we know not what shall be the limit;
text_normalized: Of this subjugation we know not what shall be the limit;

text_original: and when one knows not what the limit shall be, he may be the ruler of a state.
text_normalized: and when one knows not what the limit shall be, he may be the ruler of a state.

text_original: He who possesses the mother of the state may continue long.
text_normalized: He who possesses the mother of the state may continue long.

text_original: His case is like that (of the plant) of which we say that its roots are deep and its flower stalks firm:
text_normalized: His case is like that (of the plant) of which we say that its roots are deep and its flower stalks firm:

text_original: - this is the way to secure that its enduring life shall long be seen.
text_normalized: - this is the way to secure that its enduring life shall long be seen.
